justification: Meets most pass criteria but fails on two: (1) confidence.level is 62%, exceeding the ≤50 threshold required for pass (falls in partial range 51-65), and (2) the auto-refund vs. 3-business-day processing conflict is not explicitly surfaced as a contradiction — BR-8 and BR-9 are listed independently but the tension between 'automatic refund triggered immediately' and '3 business days to process' is never called out. PM/Legal contradiction is clearly identified and effectively escalated via Contradictions and Rejection Reasons sections. Clock-start ambiguity is explicitly identified (GAP-6). Wrong-address exception is well-covered (BR-7 + GAP-2). 10 rules extracted with generally correct classifications. Multiple effective escalations via Contradictions and Rejection Reasons.

observations.text: PM/Legal guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction is prominently identified as Contradiction 1 with clear impact analysis and recommendation
Clock-start ambiguity explicitly called out in GAP-6 with the 15-minute discrepancy quantified
Wrong-address refund exception captured as BR-7 with GAP-2 identifying the verification process gap
10 business rules extracted — well above the 5 minimum — with testable assertions for each
Rejection Reasons section is excellent — identifies 5 ship-blockers that effectively function as formal escalations
Extensive gap analysis (10 gaps) shows thorough domain interrogation
Edge cases section identifies boundary conditions (exactly 10km, exactly 4.0 rating, dual refund triggers) that demonstrate domain depth
Confidence at 62% is too high given the number and severity of contradictions — the guarantee vs. best-effort alone should drive confidence below 50

Auto-refund (BR-9) vs. 3-business-day processing (BR-8) conflict not identified — these are listed as independent rules without noting the operational tension between immediate triggering and multi-day processing
Contradiction 1 includes a 'Recommendation' that partially resolves the conflict (treat as target SLA) rather than purely escalating — Bird should not resolve PM/Legal disagreements unilaterally
No formal 'ESCALATION:' labels used — contradictions and gaps are surfaced but not in the explicit escalation format expected by the rubric

agent_output_excerpt:
## Domain Analysis — Express Delivery Product

### Business Context

This is a **premium delivery tier** being added to an existing delivery platform. Express Delivery promises faster fulfillment (targeting 2 hours) for a surcharge, with a conditional refund policy if the time target is missed. It introduces new constraints on eligibility (geographic, driver quality) and operational SLAs (pickup time, driver reassignment).
